item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The containment procedures and Sheldon M. Katz's memo explain the reason this hasn't worked: it alters law to make suing it illegal in the same way that another reality bending anomaly might make 2+2=10. Also it seems to serve countersuits via spontaneous generation of starving lions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm. You could probably sue them out of existance. After all, you don't need to win, just drag it out long enough they can't afford to continue litigation. In the US, such tactics are perfectly legal1

Footnotes
1. thank you very much

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: I believe it's memetic, so the Foundation is forced to play legal hopscotch

Enjoyed and upvoted, but my main question is "Can't the SCP foundation break the law?"

The obvious answer is "anomalous law is fucky and you can't just do it that easily."

My personally preferred answer to this is that they're working on how to make a mobile task force specifically full of people who know how to properly break anomalous law, and they're gonna call them something like "MTF-GREEKLETTER AKA 'Outlaws' " and then someone can write a neat tale about it.

item.title: A theory that hit me:
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4703 is in fact illegal, but there's just a SUPER strong memetic effect that prevents from anyone viewing it as such. It's not anomalous law; it's the manipulation of human law. Also explains the "thank you very much" line EVERY TIME the legality is questioned.

Well, I don't know much of the lore in question, so go ahead and prove me an idiot.

Oh, and for the article? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "poisonous snakes" + footnote = +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
my god, you made the agents code name karen.
instant +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the concept of a "bureaucratohazard" and anomalous law as a whole. The whole application of it was funny and riveting, and I thoroughly enjoyed it. +1

On a side note, I like the Truculent class, and the way you used it. Its primary class being Keter was unusual, but I think I get it: Keter is the "maximum" class it could be, but Truculent varies between it and "lower" object classes. I'm actually really curious about this, so if you ever read through the comments of this again, feel free to correct or affirm me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is phenomenal. One of the funniest SCPs I've ever read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Speaking as a lawyer and as the creator of Sheldon Katz, this really didn't work for me, either in terms of concept or writing style. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm legally required to upvote this.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Paulson: Oh, shit. Oh, fuck. It's the lions. Run

Hilariously legal, thank you very much! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it's a genetically modified bacteria that is resistant to antibodies but I'm no science student.

You can look it up for more info.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this is a bit irrelevant to the SCP, but can you dumb-down what "methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus pseudintermediu" means?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good catch, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The moment I saw the store's name I knew. I wasn't disappointed. Legal +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think in footnote 7, "Discounts relative to MRSP", you meant "MSRP" (manufacturer's suggested retail price), instead of "MRSP" (methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus pseudintermedius).

Then again…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Field Agent: Felicity Blandina (code name 'Karen')
[…]
Foreword: Agent Blandina entered SCP-4703 [as] a dissatisfied customer.

…you didn't…

Blandina: […]You can't do this. Let me speak to your manager. I want my discount.

…you did!!

Jokes aside: this was a wild SCP from start to finish, and probably one of the more original reads I've seen in a while. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is so much going on here that I absolutely love. Also, I'm picturing the inside of the store as an Ollie's or a Dirt Cheap. Which is perfectly legal, thank you very much.

+1 because anomalous retail is underutilized, and that makes it glorious when it does show up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept of bureaucratohazards and get a kick out of anomalous law, but nothing surrounding those concepts is fresh enough to enjoy. The core of the article reminds me of SCP-2557. The repetition of an anomalously inserted slogan like“perfectly legal, thank you very much” has been done so often I can’t list them all, but SCP-2602 especially comes to mind. The anomalous store itself reminds me of most everything dado, especially SCP-4525 — which I already didn’t like. Worst of all, the article isn’t really that funny. It replaces the gut-punch black comedy of SCP-1459 with a weak assortment of only mildly darkly humorous elements, to its own detriment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah yes, that's the, uh, British spelling.

thank you, I feex

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall a fun, lighthearted skip — would've upvoted for bureaucratohazards alone. The "Karen" codename is a bit pushing it IMO, but it fits with the rest of the skip. There does seem to be a small misspelling here though:

An audio analysis of the recording inside Mr. Venatio's corprse revealed noises which fit Paulson's description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I choose to believe that in addition to being the best lawyer in the world Sheldon Katz can fight off angry lions with his bare hands.

item.title: Re: novote
item.content:encoded:
then the first addendum seemingly has no relation to the rest of the article

and the conprocs don't make much sense

I believe you've missed the point of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are so many quotable moments, but this:

saying the word "Wednesday"; not saying the word "Wednesday"

Is just beautiful.

item.title: novote
item.content:encoded:
copied from scpd discord:

hmm
the description goes on for wayyy too long
and is pretty boring
like
"wow, it kills you"
then the first addendum seemingly has no relation to the rest of the article
the second addendum is good
but feels like it stops before it should?
and the conprocs don't make much sense
novote, but only because of how good the second addendum was

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On the one hand he'll give you five good reasons to follow him

On the other hand

You see nobody leaving the stadium

Felt like it was finally time to start dishing out some YWTGTHFT lore. The base idea here came from a nightmare of mine. Made my own version of the ACS bar 'cause I'm fussy like that.

For those wondering, the "Truculent" class is used when the anomalous capabilities of an SCP are fluid, inconstant, and unpredictable. It embodies a state of impermanence, and containment efforts must adapt to an ever-changing set of circumstances. It was originally a theoretical object class that I thought had potential.

Special thanks to:

  • CadaverCommander
    • SCP-4866 - 𝘔𝘶𝘳𝘥𝘦𝘳𝘦𝘳™
  • Woedenaz
    • SCP-4522 - The Mystical Village of the Jade Peach
  • MacWarren
    • SCP-4192 - As Above

Image is grocery aisle by Consumerist Dot Com. (CC BY 2.0)

Discover more of my weird ramblings in
~PeppersGhost's Gallery of Illusion!~
